columns.description: America/New_York calendar date derived from the authoritative complete Bronze manifest completed_at; it partitions this current-timeline capture and is not the event effective date.
columns.name: source_capture_date

columns.description: Exact unmodified provider events[].date string retained before strict parsing.
columns.name: event_date_raw

columns.description: Strict ISO-date parse of event_date_raw; this is a provider event-effective date candidate, never an announcement or research-availability timestamp.
columns.name: event_date

columns.description: Deterministic domain: ordinary_calendar_date, provider_sentinel_candidate_1969_12_31, source_boundary_candidate_2003_09_10, or provider_cluster_candidate_2023_11_18.
columns.name: event_date_quality

columns.description: True exactly when event_date is Saturday or Sunday; S5 preserves the calendar date and never shifts it to a trading session.
columns.name: event_date_is_weekend

columns.description: True exactly for the reviewed concentrated dates 1969-12-31, 2003-09-10, or 2023-11-18; the flag is diagnostic and cannot set an identity interval.
columns.name: event_date_is_known_cluster

columns.description: True when the same response Composite FIGI has more than one distinct nonblank provider ticker on event_date; S5 preserves every occurrence and chooses no ordering.
columns.name: same_figi_date_multiple_tickers

columns.description: Exact provider events[].type; the formal approved source plan requires ticker_change.
columns.name: event_type

columns.description: Exact case-sensitive provider ticker_change.ticker retained without trim, case conversion, suffix parsing, or alias merging; it does not imply an observed prior ticker.
columns.name: effective_ticker

columns.description: Fixed composite_figi for the formal authoritative receipt; ticker and CUSIP pilot requests are not admitted.
columns.name: requested_identifier_type

columns.description: Exact case-sensitive Composite FIGI sent in the authoritative request.
columns.name: requested_identifier

columns.description: Provider results.name preserved verbatim from the complete response.
columns.name: response_name

columns.description: Optional provider results.cik preserved verbatim; missing CIK is never filled from current metadata or used alone to merge share classes.
columns.name: response_cik

columns.description: Provider results.composite_figi preserved verbatim and required to equal requested_identifier.
columns.name: response_composite_figi

columns.description: Fixed evidence_only_pending_s7 marker: this row is identity evidence only and cannot itself create asset_id, issuer_id, or a ticker interval.
columns.name: identity_evidence_scope

columns.description: Always false in S5; only a separately approved S7 identity release may grant backtest identity eligibility.
columns.name: backtest_identity_eligible

columns.description: Authoritative complete Bronze manifest completed_at timestamp for the response containing this event.
columns.name: source_capture_at_utc

columns.description: First XNYS session whose open is strictly after source_capture_at_utc.
columns.name: source_available_session

columns.description: XNYS open timestamp for source_available_session; event_date never substitutes for this operational availability.
columns.name: source_available_at_utc

columns.description: Fixed first_xnys_open_after_source_capture_v1 rule.
columns.name: source_availability_rule

columns.description: Fixed current_complete_timeline_without_historical_vintage_v1 marker; historical events were observed in one current capture, not archived vintages.
columns.name: source_availability_quality

columns.description: Deterministic SHA-256 over ticker_events, source request, manifest/artifact identity, page sequence, original event ordinal, event hash, and enclosing result hash.
columns.name: source_record_id

columns.description: Canonical local Bronze request ID from the formal authoritative manifest.
columns.name: source_request_id

columns.description: Server-assigned request ID from the successful Massive response envelope.
columns.name: source_provider_request_id

columns.description: SHA-256 of the exact authoritative Bronze manifest used by this row.
columns.name: source_manifest_sha256

columns.description: SHA-256 of the stored gzip Bronze page containing this event.
columns.name: source_artifact_sha256

columns.description: Zero-based page sequence from the manifest-bound provider response.
columns.name: source_page_sequence

columns.description: Zero-based ordinal in the unmodified results.events array; it preserves source order even when the array is reverse chronological.
columns.name: source_event_ordinal

columns.description: SHA-256 of canonical JSON for the unmodified provider event object.
columns.name: source_event_hash

columns.description: SHA-256 of canonical JSON for the enclosing unmodified provider results object.
columns.name: source_result_hash

columns.description: Auditable relative Bronze artifact path plus page and events-array ordinal for this occurrence.
columns.name: source_pointer

description: Formal Massive ticker-change event occurrences retained as current-capture, evidence-only identity inputs with explicit event-date quality, conservative operational availability, high-quarantine blank targets, and complete Bronze lineage; no row creates an asset ID, prior ticker, effective trading-session interval, or backtest eligibility.
grain: One structurally valid manifest-bound occurrence in results.events from one formal successful Composite-FIGI ticker_events request; source duplicates and array ordinals remain distinct, while an invalid blank target occurrence is represented only by a high-severity quarantine record.

qa_rules.description: Every output Parquet field, order, Arrow type, and nullability must equal this contract.
The immutable 15,173-row formal Composite-FIGI receipt and exact request definitions must reconcile with no missing, extra, duplicate, or pilot request.
No admitted complete manifest or manifest-bound artifact may fail status, path, bytes, checksum, gzip, JSON, page sequence, or declared event-count validation.
Every admitted successful page must have an OK envelope, object results, nonblank provider request ID, and the reviewed results.name/composite_figi/events shape.
Every event must descend from a formal request for one Composite FIGI with types=ticker_change and the exact frozen local request labels.
No successful response Composite FIGI may be missing, blank, or differ byte-for-byte from its requested formal Composite FIGI.
No accepted DATA row may have a non-object event, non-ISO date, type other than ticker_change, non-object ticker_change payload, or non-string target.
Every accepted event_date_raw must parse strictly and round-trip exactly to event_date.
Formal event occurrences must reconcile exactly to accepted DATA occurrences, exact duplicate excess, and quarantined occurrences; valid siblings of invalid placeholders remain accepted.
The output must contain no duplicate source_record_id.
Every row must reproduce its request, manifest, artifact, page, original event ordinal, event/result hashes, source pointer, and source_record_id.
Capture date/time and operational availability must follow the frozen calendar and first-XNYS-open-strictly-after-capture rule.
Every event_date_quality must be reproduced exactly from event_date using the four-value reviewed domain.
Weekend, known-cluster, and same-FIGI/date-multiple-ticker flags must be deterministic and must not change source values.
Every row must retain the exact evidence_only_pending_s7 scope marker.
No S5 event row may be marked backtest identity eligible.
Every event source_request_id must have exactly one formal complete parent in ticker_event_request_status.
No isolated pilot request or pilot event may enter DATA, QA denominators for formal coverage, or quarantine.
A response containing a blank placeholder must retain every other structurally valid event occurrence with its original ordinal.
No missing, empty, or whitespace-only ticker_change.ticker may enter DATA; each such occurrence must produce one high-severity quarantine record.
No accepted target ticker may contain leading or trailing whitespace; exact internal case and punctuation remain unmodified.
Known blank target occurrences are counted before high-severity quarantine so the 193-row provider placeholder population remains explicit.
Successful formal responses without results.cik remain valid FIGI evidence and are counted without CIK backfill.
Events dated 1969-12-31 remain visible as provider-sentinel candidates and cannot independently begin an identity interval.
Events exactly on the local request start label remain visible as source-boundary candidates; the label was not a server date filter.
All events dated 2023-11-18 remain visible as a reviewed provider date cluster, including valid targets and quarantined blanks.
Weekend event dates are preserved without shifting and require S7 membership corroboration before defining a trading-session boundary.
Composite-FIGI/date groups containing multiple distinct target tickers remain unresolved evidence with no source-order winner.
Exact tickers observed under multiple Composite FIGIs remain separate identity evidence and must never be ticker-merged.
Composite FIGIs observed with multiple target tickers remain visible for S7 lifecycle construction.
Events before the S4 membership window remain uncorroborated by S4 and cannot be described as PIT-validated aliases.
Events after the local request end label are preserved because ticker-events start/end labels were not server-side filters.
Future-effective events relative to source capture, if present, remain scheduled evidence and cannot be applied before event_date.
Repeated Composite-FIGI/date/type/effective-ticker semantic keys are preserved as source occurrences and surfaced rather than silently deduplicated.
Multi-event responses not in the predominant reverse-chronological source order remain visible; transformations preserve ordinal and never infer semantics from array order.
Events or response objects containing fields outside the reviewed mapping remain source-visible and require schema review before publication.
